encounter.reason: new patient psychiatric evaluation for continuity of care; bipolar II disorder and alcohol use disorder management
encounter.location: Puget Sound Psychiatry, 1001 4th Ave, Suite 820, Seattle, WA 98154

labs.name: triglycerides
labs.flag: mildly elevated (consistent with prior trend on quetiapine)

conditions.name: alcohol use disorder, moderate severity, in sustained remission

medications.name: lamotrigine 200 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 200 mg by mouth once daily

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 50 mg by mouth at bedtime

medications.name: naltrexone 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take 50 mg by mouth once daily

document_references.description: Comprehensive 60-minute new patient psychiatric evaluation documenting full psychiatric history (bipolar II diagnosis, medication journey from sertraline to lamotrigine/quetiapine, alcohol use disorder treatment and recovery), mental status examination (euthymic, linear thought process, no SI/HI), risk assessment (low acute risk), medication reconciliation, screening results (PHQ-9 4, GAD-7 3), baseline lab results, diagnostic formulation, and treatment plan with 3-month follow-up.

diagnostic_reports.description: Lipid panel ordered for baseline metabolic monitoring; only triglycerides value (158 mg/dL, mildly elevated) is individually reported in the narrative. Other lipid panel components (total cholesterol, LDL, HDL) are not enumerated. Triglycerides consistent with prior trend on quetiapine.
Metabolic panel ordered for baseline monitoring; only fasting glucose (88 mg/dL, normal) is individually reported. Remaining metabolic panel components are not enumerated in the narrative.
